rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ALICE JACKSON et al VS. ROSE L. JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 29, 2008, line 1. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT is denied. Plaintiff has not been personally served. See Cedars-Sinai Imaging Medical Group v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 1281, 1286. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA ALICE JACKSON et al VS. ROSE L. JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 29, 2008, line 1. PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA JACKSON AND MARIAN JACKSON-CHAVEZ'S Motion For Judgment is denied. Prejudgment interest has not and is not being sought by Rose L. Jackson. The Order for Apportionment was the judgment in this case. See Passavanti v. Williams (1990) 225 Cal. App. 3d 1602, 1605-06. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GERARD MOONEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 29, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT PCC FLOW TECHNOLOGIES L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof. Plaintiff's interrogatory answers detail worksites where he allegedly was exposed to asbestos from defendant's Paco products. Plaintiff identifies a discrete number of witnesses with some contact information and defendant's deposition testimony expands on his exposure to Paco. Plaintiff's testimony is not subject to a motion to strike under Dicola v. White Brother Performance Products (2008) 15 Cal. App. 4th 66. Defendant has presented no evidence to suggest that defendant's product was not in the packages seen by plaintiff. Plaintiff's declaration along with the Ay declaration also create a triable issue of fact and the objections to those declarations are overruled. The motion for summary adjudication is granted on the false representation, negligent misrepresentation and punitive damages claim. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BERNARD ROGOVER VS. A. P. GREEN INDUSTRIES, INC. (A.P. GRREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 29, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT T H AGRICULTURE & NUTRITION, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: BERNARD ROGOVER VS. A. P. GREEN INDUSTRIES, INC. (A.P. GRREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 29, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT DAP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. Plaintiff has testified to using defendant's product as far back as 1977 (Depo. 756). Defendant acknowledges certain of its products contained asbestos. The Reid declaration refers to wet and gooey formulations as possibly containing asbestos and plaintiff's interrogatory answers describe the product in that manner. Assuming the burden has shifted, plaintiff's declaration is not subject to a motion to strike under D'Amico and creates a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: WADE ANTHONY ROBERTSON VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 29, 2008, line 11. PETITIONER WADE ROBERTSON'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate is denied. If there was a problem with the administrative record at the time of the first writ hearing on the suspension of petitioner's driver's license, petitioner needed to raise the issue at or before the hearing. The Court already has ruled on the merits of D M V's revocation of petitioner's license. =(302/PJM/AY)
